_meta.title: CAN SLIM Tactical Momentum Engine — Technical Specification Ledger
_meta.author: William J. O'Neil (How to Make Money in Stocks, 4th Edition)
_meta.purpose: Authoritative source of truth for all quantitative momentum thresholds, volume multipliers, and adaptive market regime toggles for fw_can_slim in scoring_engine.py and can_slim_pass output column.
_meta.design_rationale: CAN SLIM is a tactical timing layer above the structural quality frameworks. While Coffee Can and Diamonds identify what to own, CAN SLIM identifies when to deploy — specifically at breakout pivots where institutional accumulation is confirmed, earnings are accelerating on both bottom and top line, and the market regime provides macro tailwind. The M criterion is the only framework in the platform that uses internal breadth consensus (detect_market_regime) to dynamically block new entries during bear phases.
_meta.implementation_note: All 7 criteria are implementable with StockScan CSV data. A criterion annual consistency uses eps_gr_3y >= 0 + eps_gr_yoy >= 0 as the best available proxy for O'Neil's 3-consecutive-year unbroken increase requirement (year-by-year EPS back-series not in CSV). M criterion uses detect_market_regime(df) internal breadth consensus — not external market index data. v2.1 adds two bonus score components: (1) EPS annual acceleration (eps_gr_yoy > eps_gr_3y) and (2) OPM expansion (opm > opm_1yb) — both derived from O'Neil Ch.3+4 textbook text; both score-only, not hard gates.

c_current_quarterly_growth._purpose: Confirms earnings AND revenue are both accelerating in the most recent reported quarter.

quarterly_eps_growth.rationale: O'Neil Chapter 3: quarterly EPS (per share) must expand >= 25% YoY. Best-in-class winners show 40-100%+. Uses q_eps_yoy = (eps_lq - eps_pyq) / abs(eps_pyq) * 100, precomputed in data_engine.py with zero-base guard at source. EPS per share (not total PAT) is the canonical O'Neil metric — accounts for dilution effects. fillna(0) fails conservatively.
quarterly_eps_growth.precomputed_in: data_engine.py — q_eps_yoy (eps_lq vs eps_pyq, zero-base guarded)

quarterly_sales_growth.rationale: O'Neil Chapter 3: quarterly EPS spikes are dangerous value traps unless validated by concurrent top-line revenue acceleration >= 25%. Without sales confirmation, earnings growth is suspect (cost-cutting, one-time items, tax reversals). fillna(0) fails conservatively — missing quarterly revenue data cannot confirm organic demand.
quarterly_sales_growth.fillna_strategy: fillna(0) — unknown quarterly sales data fails conservatively

eps_annual_acceleration.rationale: O'Neil Chapter 3+4: 'The rate of earnings increase should be getting larger in each of the past few quarters or years — look for two or three periods of accelerating earnings growth.' eps_gr_yoy > eps_gr_3y means the MOST RECENT annual EPS growth rate is HIGHER than the 3-year CAGR baseline, confirming the business is in an acceleration phase rather than mean-reverting toward a prior growth rate. Score bonus only — not a fw_can_slim hard gate. A strong business can briefly dip below its 3Y baseline due to a high-base effect while still growing 30%+ YoY.
eps_annual_acceleration.code_location: scoring_engine.py — can_slim_score (bonus, not fw_can_slim hard gate)
eps_annual_acceleration.known_limitation: Uses annual EPS growth rates as proxy. O'Neil's pure acceleration signal uses sequential quarterly YoY comparisons (Q_n growth > Q_(n-1) growth) which requires EPS 1 Quarter Back, EPS 2 Quarters Back — absent from user's current CSV exports (see missing_csv_columns_gap). Annual proxy is directionally correct but less granular.

a_annual_earnings_increases._purpose: Verifies sustained multi-year earnings compounding with capital efficiency — eliminates cyclical recoveries.

annual_eps_cagr_5y.rationale: O'Neil Chapter 4: annual earnings must show significant increase. 25% is the floor for elite growers — average winning stocks showed 25-50% annual EPS growth before breakout. fillna(0) fails conservatively.

return_on_equity.rationale: O'Neil Chapter 4: ROE >= 17% is the explicit capital efficiency threshold separating high-quality compounders from leveraged mediocres. Note: Minervini (SEPA) uses same 17% threshold — both derived from O'Neil original research. Coffee Can uses 15%; Diamonds does not explicitly test ROE. fillna(0) conservative.

annual_consistency_eps_no_decline.rationale: O'Neil Chapter 4: specifies 3 consecutive years of unbroken annual earnings increases. CSV does not provide year-by-year EPS back-series. Best available proxy: eps_gr_3y >= 0 (overall 3Y CAGR positive — net trajectory is upward) AND eps_gr_yoy >= 0 (not currently declining in the most recent year). A company with both positive 3Y CAGR AND positive YoY growth almost certainly has 3 consecutive positive years. fillna(-1) fails conservatively — missing history cannot be assumed consistent.
annual_consistency_eps_no_decline.known_limitation: Book requires year-by-year check for each of 3 years. CSV provides CAGR and YoY only. The combined gate (3Y CAGR >= 0 AND YoY >= 0) approximates this — a company with positive 3Y CAGR and positive current YoY very likely had 3 consecutive positive years. Severity: LOW.

pat_step_growth_3y.rationale: O'Neil Chapter 4: direct year-by-year profit increase verification. Complements the EPS CAGR proxy (eps_gr_3y/eps_gr_yoy) with raw step-growth confirmation. NaN comparisons in pandas return False — missing back-year data conservatively fails the gate. Known limitation: uses PAT (total profit), not EPS per share — does not account for share dilution. EPS-based proxies above remain as the dilution-adjusted consistency check.

operating_margin_expansion.rationale: O'Neil Chapter 4: 'Look for annual pre-tax profit margins at new peak levels. Companies with profit margins improving and at new highs have pricing power and cost discipline — not just revenue-driven earnings.' opm > opm_1yb confirms operating margin is expanding YoY (new-peak direction). Score bonus only — not a fw_can_slim hard gate. Some capital-intensive businesses invest heavily in a given year, temporarily compressing margins while building a multi-year earnings base.
operating_margin_expansion.code_location: scoring_engine.py — can_slim_score (bonus, not fw_can_slim hard gate)
operating_margin_expansion.fillna_strategy: fillna(0) on both opm_cs and opm_1yb_cs — missing data treated as both = 0 (equal, not expanding), so bonus does not fire

n_new_highs_and_catalysts._chapter: Chapter 5 — N: New Highs, New Products, New Management
n_new_highs_and_catalysts._purpose: Positions entry near structural breakout pivots — eliminates stocks languishing in deep bases.

distance_from_52w_high_pct.rationale: O'Neil Chapter 5: 'buy at or near 52-week highs — within 10-15%'. A stock within 15% of its 52-week high is in a strong base or breaking out. Stocks deep in bases (>15% below highs) have overhead supply that creates resistance. fillna(999) fails conservatively — missing price history = unknown distance.

s_supply_and_demand._purpose: Confirms institutional block-buying via volume surge above baseline average. Secondary: float retraction confirms structural supply constraint.

volume_ratio_sma20.rationale: O'Neil Chapter 6: volume must be 40-50% above average on breakout days to confirm institutions are accumulating. vol_ratio >= 1.5 = volume is 50%+ above 20D SMA — meets the upper end of O'Neil's range. fillna(1.0) neutral — missing volume data treated as normal volume (passes at margin, not rewarded).

float_retraction_buyback.rationale: O'Neil Chapter 6: companies with fewer shares outstanding (via buybacks or no dilution) have a structural supply constraint that aids price appreciation. equity_shares <= equity_shares_1yb confirms current float has not expanded — share count stable or declining. Both values must be present; missing data fails conservatively (no buyback assumed). Added to can_slim_score only — primary S hard gate remains volume surge.
float_retraction_buyback.code_location: scoring_engine.py — fw_can_slim can_slim_score (S2-bonus, not fw_can_slim hard gate)

vcp_volume_contraction_pattern.rationale: O'Neil Chapter 6 + Minervini SEPA VCP adaptation: the highest-conviction institutional breakouts are preceded by a period of volume contraction (supply silently absorbed) followed by explosive volume expansion on the pivot breakout day. vol_sma_5d < vol_sma_20d confirms the recent 5-day average is below the 20-day baseline — volume has been drying up in the base. vol_ratio >= 1.5 confirms the current session volume is surging ≥ 50% above the 20D average. The combination (quiet base + loud breakout) is the VCP signature. Both SMA series must be present and positive — missing data fails conservatively. Score bonus only — not a fw_can_slim hard gate.
vcp_volume_contraction_pattern.code_location: scoring_engine.py — can_slim_score (S3-bonus, not fw_can_slim hard gate)
vcp_volume_contraction_pattern.data_source: vol_sma_5d and vol_sma_20d mapped in TECHNICAL_COLS; vol_ratio derived in data_engine.py

l_leader_vs_laggard._purpose: Filters for the top 20% of the equity universe by multi-timeframe relative price strength.

relative_strength_percentile.rationale: O'Neil Chapter 7: 'RS Rating of 80 or higher — average winning stock has RS Rating of 87 before breakout'. d47_rs_composite uses IBD-authentic weighting: most recent period (crs_50d ≈ 10W) receives 40% weight, mid-term (crs_26w = 6M) 30%, long-term (crs_52w = 12M) 30%. This replicates IBD's double-weighting of the most recent quarter. Percentile-ranked across all 2,108 stocks — top 20% = RS Rating >= 80. fillna(50) neutral — stocks with missing RS data score at median.
relative_strength_percentile.formula_location: data_engine.py — D47 d47_rs_composite (IBD-weighted, not simple average)

rs_line_uptrend_multiframe.rationale: O'Neil Chapter 7: The RS LINE should be trending upward — ideally making new highs before the price does. rs_pctrank_cs >= 80 measures WHERE the RS line is (level). This signal measures DIRECTION: crs_50d > crs_26w > crs_52w means 50-day relative strength exceeds 26-week which exceeds 52-week — the RS line is ascending across all three timeframes. A stock with rising RS line + already at 80th percentile is demonstrably stronger than a stock stuck at 80th percentile with a flat or declining RS line. All three CRS series must be present; missing any one fails conservatively (direction unconfirmed = no bonus). Score bonus only — complements the L1 hard gate (level + direction together).
rs_line_uptrend_multiframe.code_location: scoring_engine.py — can_slim_score (L2-bonus, not fw_can_slim hard gate)
rs_line_uptrend_multiframe.data_source: crs_50d, crs_26w, crs_52w mapped in TECHNICAL_COLS (CRS Vs Nifty 500 50D/26W/52W)

i_institutional_sponsorship._purpose: Confirms smart-money accumulation — FII or DII buying in the latest reported quarter.

institutional_accumulation.rationale: O'Neil Chapter 8: A-rated institutional accumulation (A-D A/D rating). Best India-specific proxy: FII or DII increased holdings in latest quarter. change_fii_lq > 0 OR change_dii_lq > 0. fillna(0) neutral — missing quarter-change data treated as no change (fails this gate).

m_market_direction_integration._purpose: Blocks new CAN SLIM breakout entries during confirmed bear regimes to preserve capital.

market_regime_gate.rationale: O'Neil Chapter 9: '3 out of 4 stocks follow the direction of the overall market'. Buying growth stocks in a bear market leads to immediate capital drawdown regardless of fundamental quality. The internal detect_market_regime(df) breadth consensus (2/3 of crs_50d > 60%, crs_26w > 55%, above_sma200 > 55%) maps directly to O'Neil's market direction methodology. When regime == BEAR: fw_can_slim = False for ALL stocks — no exceptions. BULL or SIDEWAYS: M criterion passes.

implementation_mapping._score_component_breakdown: C: 2 hard + 1 bonus; A: 5 hard + 1 bonus; N: 1; S: 1 hard + 2 bonus; L: 1 hard + 1 bonus; I: 1; M: 1

missing_csv_columns_gap._purpose: Documents columns listed in ALL_INDICATOR_AVAILABLES (STOCKSCAN).csv that are not present in the user's 6 Screener.in CSV exports and affect CAN SLIM precision.

quarterly_eps_back_series.impact: Cannot compute O'Neil's primary C criterion acceleration signal (sequential quarterly YoY EPS comparison). Mitigated by annual acceleration proxy: eps_gr_yoy > eps_gr_3y.
quarterly_eps_back_series.action: Add these columns to Screener.in CSV export to enable quarterly acceleration gate.

quarterly_revenue_back_series.impact: Cannot compute quarterly revenue acceleration (sequential YoY comparison). Mitigated: q_rev_yoy >= 25 hard gate plus annual rev_gr_yoy already mapped.
quarterly_revenue_back_series.action: Add to CSV export to enable revenue acceleration check.

eps_growth_qoq.impact: Pre-computed QoQ growth rates absent. Same mitigation as quarterly back-series above.

known_gaps.gap: A criterion: 3-consecutive-year EPS increase requires year-by-year annual EPS back-series
known_gaps.mitigation: eps_gr_3y >= 0 AND eps_gr_yoy >= 0 combined gate. A company with positive 3Y CAGR and positive current YoY almost certainly had 3 consecutive positive years. The only edge case where this proxy fails is a V-shaped recovery (decline in year 2, massive recovery in year 3 that lifts the CAGR positive despite a mid-period dip). Such V-shapes are rare among stocks already screening >= 25% 5Y CAGR.

known_gaps.gap: I criterion: FII/DII quarter-change is a 90-day lag vs O'Neil's real-time A-D rating
known_gaps.mitigation: No real-time institutional data in CSV. Quarterly change is the best available proxy and is still directionally correct — rising FII/DII in the most recent reported quarter is consistent with institutional accumulation.

known_gaps.gap: C criterion: quarterly EPS acceleration (sequential Q-by-Q YoY comparison) requires back-quarter EPS series absent from current CSV exports
known_gaps.mitigation: Annual acceleration proxy: eps_gr_yoy > eps_gr_3y (v2.1 addition). Resolve by adding EPS 1/2/3 Quarter Back to Screener.in CSV export.
